creator: sasaki12@gmail.com
text:
Hello,

I have checked the latest version.

I think this will work under almost all circumstances. However, this still kills unrelated processes under a particular circumstance, such as running xymon-client under both host and jail environment. I think this is a rare case, but I do this actually because I want to see the processes separately.

I attached the new patch that runs 'pkill -g PGID' after stopping xymonlaunch. I'm not sure this is the smart way, but this works for me.
